findings.title: North Star Metric: Accessibility Compliance Rate
findings.description: The Accessibility Compliance Rate measures the percentage of pages that meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards, reflecting the overall accessibility of the LearnPath platform for users with disabilities.
assumptions: Improving accessibility will lead to higher user satisfaction and engagement.

evidence.excerpt: Pages passing AA: 2 out of 15 audited.

recommendations: Increase the number of pages audited and improve the design to meet WCAG standards.

metadata.measurement_method: Percentage of pages passing WCAG 2.1 AA standards

findings.title: Primary KPI: Screen Reader Task Completion Rate
findings.description: Measures the percentage of tasks completed by users utilizing screen readers, indicating the effectiveness of the platform's accessibility features.
assumptions: Users with disabilities will complete tasks if accessibility is improved.
recommendations: Enhance screen reader compatibility for key tasks.

metadata.measurement_method: Percentage of tasks completed by screen reader users

findings.title: Primary KPI: Keyboard-Only Task Completion Rate
findings.description: Measures the percentage of tasks completed by users navigating with a keyboard only, reflecting the accessibility for motor-impaired users.
assumptions: Improving keyboard navigation will increase task completion rates.
recommendations: Improve keyboard navigation across the platform.

metadata.measurement_method: Percentage of tasks completed by keyboard-only users

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: WCAG Violations Count
findings.description: Tracks the total number of WCAG violations across the platform, ensuring that the number does not increase as improvements are made.
assumptions: Reducing WCAG violations will improve overall user experience.
recommendations: Regularly audit and address WCAG violations.

findings.title: Measurement Gap: User Feedback on Accessibility
findings.description: Currently, there is insufficient qualitative data on user experiences regarding accessibility features, which limits understanding of user needs.
assumptions: User feedback will provide insights into accessibility improvements.

evidence.excerpt: No user feedback collected specifically on accessibility features.

recommendations: Implement user surveys focused on accessibility experiences.

findings.title: Experiment Opportunity: Accessibility Feature Testing
findings.description: Conduct A/B testing on different accessibility feature implementations to determine which changes yield the highest improvements in task completion rates.
assumptions: Different implementations will have varying impacts on user experience.
recommendations: Design and execute A/B tests for accessibility features.

summary: The analysis identifies a North Star metric focused on Accessibility Compliance Rate, with supporting KPIs for screen reader and keyboard-only task completion rates. Guardrail metrics track WCAG violations, while gaps in user feedback and opportunities for A/B testing are highlighted to enhance accessibility efforts.
